rationale.R1.1: The artifact contains an explicit gap statement (single-forward-pass kinematic failures), a contribution claim (closed-loop critique-and-revise architecture), and an implicit thesis; evaluating whether these are coherent and well-framed is a natural responsibility when asked to 'look at' a research excerpt.
rationale.R1.2: The text chains a failure-mode analysis into a proposed solution and a success-rate claim; checking whether the logical steps (open-loop baseline → instrumented failure breakdown → iterative critic → improved success) cohere is central to any substantive review.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are present (240 trials, six tasks, 41%/27% failure breakdown, 38%→71% success lift); these must be traceable to and consistent with the stated experimental setup, making evidence-claim alignment a core check.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation 'take a look at this paragraph' is the canonical phrasing that triggers a writing-quality pass; sentence construction, paragraph flow, and clarity are plausibly the primary intent.
